name: Stuffed Rabbit
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dry the rabbit thoroughly.
2. Chop the onions and gently fry in 2 ounces of butter until softened.
3. Peel and chop the cooking apples, then add them to the onions. Continue frying until the apples are soft.
4. Mix the cooked onions, apples, and remaining 2 ounces of butter with all other stuffing ingredients (breadcrumbs, parsley, thyme, sugar, salt).
5. Shape the mixture into a large enough portion to stuff the rabbit, and quickly brown the edges in the remaining butter.
6. Place the rabbit in a casserole dish, remove the cavity if possible, and fill it generously with the prepared stuffing.
7. Surround the stuffed rabbit with any excess stuffing.
8. Add well-seasoned stock (10 fluid ounces) to the casserole, ensuring it reaches just below the top of the rabbit.
9. Cover the casserole and bake at 350°F for 1 hour and 15 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F, turning off heat when done.
Note: Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper to taste before serving.
